mechanism_narrative: Housing starts act as a leading indicator for economic activity. Rising HOUST signals stronger future demand, boosting corporate earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between 3-month change in HOUST and subsequent 60-day SPX returns is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 5% signals the onset of recession, eroding consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In more than 50% of triggered instances, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive despite UNRATE exceeding 5%.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds 3% year-over-year, further increases in the federal funds rate compress equity valuations via higher discount rates. The interaction between rising CPI and rising FEDFUNDS amplifies the negative signal.
regime_hint: monetary tightening regime with above-target inflation
failure_condition: Within triggered periods, 20-day SPX returns are positive more than 50% of the time when both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL are rising.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market with unemployment below 4%, further declines in UNRATE signal wage inflation and imminent Fed tightening, which depresses equity valuations over short horizons.
regime_hint: tight labor market regime (UNRATE below estimated NAIRU of 4.5%)
failure_condition: Over 5-day windows after UNRATE drops below 4%, SPX returns are positive more than 60% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing activity and corporate output, which boosts earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns on a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between monthly INDPRO changes and subsequent 20-day SPX returns is consistently negative over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding 1% signals sudden weakening in consumer demand, leading to negative corporate earnings surprises and downward SPX moves over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are positive in more than half of months following a >1% monthly decline in RSAFS.

mechanism_narrative: Joint declines in both employment (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) over a 3-month window signal a broad demand-led contraction, strongly predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in less than 60% of joint-decline episodes.

mechanism_narrative: During a housing boom (3-month HOUST growth >15% YoY), further increases signal construction overheating and eventual supply glut, leading to a pullback in housing-related equities and broader market negative returns over 60 days.
regime_hint: housing boom regime with rapid supply growth
failure_condition: 60-day SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered boom periods.
